title: Make compiled Squint modules executable in the browser
description:
## Problem
The build uses :elide-imports true while core.cljs and search.cljs rely on Squint runtime functions. Generated modules reference undefined squint_core and fail before initializing client features.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `just build`.
2. Inspect the start of dist/core.mjs or dist/search.mjs and note the missing runtime import.
3. Execute or load core.mjs and observe `ReferenceError: squint_core is not defined`.
design: Relevant files: squint.edn, justfile, package.json, package-lock.json, src/core.cljs, src/search.cljs, and a focused browser-smoke script. Chosen strategy: disable import elision, compile to an intermediate directory, then bundle core.cljs and search.cljs entry points with esbuild using ESM code splitting into dist. Add Playwright-based `just check-js` against a local static server; install Chromium in CI. Do not rely on a CDN or browser bare-specifier resolution.
acceptance_criteria: Squint compilation retains required runtime imports; an esbuild bundling step emits browser-resolvable static ESM for core and search, including the dynamic search import; GitHub Pages needs no bare package resolution or CDN runtime; `just check-js` serves dist and runs a Playwright smoke test that fails on page errors, console errors, or module-load failures; the smoke test is part of CI.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Unify CI deployment with the canonical site build
description:
## Problem
The Pages workflow skips episode generation, Org-page generation, redirects, search-index generation, and global-player injection. Production returns 404 for generated routes and exposes the literal GLOBAL_PLAYER placeholder. The built-site audit validates only dist/index.html.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Inspect .github/workflows/deploy.yml and compare its build step with `just build`.
2. Request /episodios/, /search-index.json, /sobre/, or /contacto/ from production.
3. Inspect the deployed homepage for the GLOBAL_PLAYER placeholder.
design: Relevant files: .github/workflows/deploy.yml, justfile, scripts/a11y-audit.mjs, scripts/check-reader-mode.mjs. CI must call repository-owned just targets rather than duplicate build commands. Add artifact assertions and recursive HTML validation. Verification: clean checkout build plus the same commands used by the Pages job.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions invokes the canonical build pipeline; a clean CI run produces episodes, Org pages, redirects, search-index.json, and injected player markup; all generated HTML files are validated; reader-mode checks cover representative generated pages; CI fails when any required artifact is absent.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Make episode search results keyboard accessible
description:
## Problem
Search results use listbox/option semantics, but src/search.cljs has no keyboard navigation, active-option management, Escape handling, or equivalent native-link interaction model. This leaves the OpenSpec keyboard-navigation requirement unmet.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Build and open a page containing episode search.
2. Type at least two characters to display results.
3. Attempt to navigate and activate results using only Arrow keys, Enter, and Escape.
design: Relevant files: src/search.cljs, resources/index.html, scripts/episode-template.html, and scripts/build-episodes.mjs. Prefer the smallest coherent ARIA model; if retaining listbox, implement active-option state and key handling, otherwise use a native list of links. Verification: just build && just check-js with keyboard-only assertions.
acceptance_criteria: Search uses one coherent accessible interaction model: either Arrow keys move an exposed active option, Enter activates it, and Escape closes results, or listbox semantics are removed and normal links are fully reachable by native Tab navigation; behavior is tested in the shared browser smoke suite; no invalid ARIA relationships remain.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Publish referenced materials assets
description:
## Problem
materiales/index.org links to materiales/raw/charly-segment-9m19s-10m19s.mp3, but the build does not copy that asset into dist, so the published link is broken.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `just build`.
2. Inspect dist/materiales/index.html for the raw MP3 link.
3. Confirm the corresponding file is absent from dist/materiales/raw/.
design: Relevant files: squint.edn or justfile resource-copy configuration, materiales/index.org, and materiales/raw/. Preserve source-relative paths in dist. Extend the artifact/link checker to inspect local href/src targets.
acceptance_criteria: The build copies referenced materials assets while preserving their relative paths; the MP3 link from dist/materiales/index.html resolves locally and in the deployed artifact; an artifact/link check fails if a referenced local asset is absent.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Generate Org pages at the pretty URLs used by navigation
description:
## Problem
Org pages are emitted as dist/paginas/<name>.html while redirects and links target /paginas/<name>/. GitHub Pages requires directory index files for these slash routes.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `just build`.
2. Compare dist/paginas/sobre.html with the /paginas/sobre/ target generated in dist/sobre/index.html.
3. Deploy to GitHub Pages and request the slash target.
design: Relevant files: scripts/build-org-pages.mjs and generated redirect templates. Emit paginas/<slug>/index.html for non-index Org sources while preserving materiales/index.org and other true index pages. Verify artifact paths locally; CI/deployment checks verify /sobre/, /contacto/, and canonical targets.
acceptance_criteria: Non-index Org sources generate dist/<path>/<slug>/index.html or links consistently target the actual output; /sobre/, /contacto/, and their canonical destinations return 200 on GitHub Pages; redirect pages do not lead to a second 404.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Correct Squint interop in audio persistence and progress updates
description:
## Problem
Session-storage forms compile to invalid chained property calls, and the range maximum update compiles as range.max.value. Persistence silently fails and progress updates can throw.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `just build`.
2. Inspect dist/core.mjs for sessionStorage.getItem.equivocadxs_audio and range_el.max.value.
3. After restoring the Squint runtime, exercise playback and state persistence and observe the failed operations.
design: Relevant file: src/core.cljs. Use direct JS method calls for Storage and assign range.max directly. Keep storage parsing failure-tolerant without hiding programming errors. Verification: just build && just check-js, covering play, pause, seek, close, reload, and malformed stored JSON.
acceptance_criteria: Session state can be saved and restored with valid getItem/setItem calls; the seek range max tracks audio duration; play, pause, seek, close, reload, and malformed-storage scenarios are covered by focused tests or a browser smoke test; no related console errors occur.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Hide unfinished production media and platform controls
description:
## Problem
Published pages expose example.com audio URLs, incomplete Spotify and Apple destinations, a placeholder YouTube video, and /feed.xml even though no feed exists. Until authoritative destinations are supplied, these controls mislead users. Supplying real URLs and publishing local materials are tracked separately.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Inspect resources/index.html and published episode frontmatter.
2. Follow the visible audio, Spotify, Apple, YouTube, and RSS controls.
3. Observe placeholder destinations or 404 responses.
design: Relevant files: resources/index.html and episodios/*.md. Remove or omit unfinished controls at their source; do not add new placeholder values or generate RSS in this ticket. Verification: just build, validate all dist HTML, and run the artifact/link checker introduced by the deployment work.
acceptance_criteria: No visible production page links to example.com audio, incomplete platform roots, the placeholder YouTube video, or a nonexistent feed.xml; unavailable controls are omitted rather than disabled; page copy remains coherent when controls are absent; generated HTML and internal-link checks pass; restoring real destinations is out of scope and tracked by the human decision ticket.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Repair episode filter markup and ARIA state handling
description:
## Problem
Filter markup emits data-tag while search.cljs reads dataset.value. Reflected ariaPressed string values are also treated as booleans.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `just build` and open the episode index with the client module working.
2. Select a filter chip.
3. Observe that the implementation reads an undefined tag and does not toggle the initial false state correctly.
design: Relevant files: src/search.cljs and scripts/build-episodes.mjs. Standardize on data-tag and explicit string-valued aria-pressed access through getAttribute/setAttribute. Verification: just build && just check-js with Todos, one tag, multiple tags, clear, and no-match cases.
acceptance_criteria: Filter code reads data-tag consistently; aria-pressed is compared and written explicitly; Todos, single-tag, multi-tag, clear, and zero-result flows show the correct episodes and accessible state; tests cover the markup/client contract.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Make player markup and client scripts consistent on every generated page
description:
## Problem
The generated episode index embeds global-player markup but omits core.mjs. Org-page interpolation consumes the GLOBAL_PLAYER placeholder before explicit player injection, leaving those pages without player markup.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `just build`.
2. Inspect dist/episodios/index.html and note the player markup without a core.mjs script.
3. Inspect a generated Org page and note the core.mjs script without global-player markup.
design: Relevant files: scripts/build-episodes.mjs, scripts/build-org-pages.mjs, scripts/episode-template.html, scripts/org-template.html, scripts/global-player.html, scripts/inject-player.mjs. Establish one placeholder-injection order and one page contract. Verification: just build; assert player markup and core.mjs each occur exactly once on every interactive page; run just check-js.
acceptance_criteria: Every page intended to support interactivity includes both the required player markup and /core.mjs exactly once; direct visits to the episode index and Org pages initialize without missing-element errors; generated output contains no unresolved or accidentally removed placeholders.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Remove partial SPA navigation and restore native navigation
description:
## Problem
Custom navigation swaps only #main-content while page-specific header state remains stale. The popstate normal branch compiles without swapping content. Native navigation is the intended correction; completing a custom SPA is out of scope.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Initialize the client module.
2. Navigate from the homepage to an episode through an intercepted internal link and compare the H1, breadcrumb, and aria-current state with the destination HTML.
3. Use browser back or forward and observe that URL and content can diverge.
design: Relevant files: src/core.cljs. Remove init-nav, navigate-to, swap-content, init-search-on-content, and custom popstate behavior rather than expanding the SPA. Keep normal links and player state restoration. Verification: just build && just check-js.
acceptance_criteria: Custom internal-link interception and popstate content loading are removed; internal links use native browser navigation including modifier keys and history; each destination presents its own H1, breadcrumb, title, navigation state, and search UI; repaired session storage preserves audio source and position across reloads; uninterrupted playback is explicitly out of scope; the browser smoke suite covers forward and back navigation.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Add podcast distribution links (Spotify, Apple, RSS)
description: Add a visible badge bar with links to Spotify, Apple Podcasts, YouTube, and RSS feed so users can subscribe via their preferred platform.
acceptance_criteria:
- Badge bar visible in header or below hero
- Icons/buttons for: Spotify, Apple Podcasts, YouTube, RSS
- Links open in new tab
- Responsive layout

title: Add inline audio player to homepage hero
description: Add an inline play button on the hero card of the homepage so users can listen to the latest episode with one click, without navigating to a subpage.
acceptance_criteria:
- Hero section shows play/pause button on latest episode
- Clicking play starts audio immediately (no navigation)
- Duration counter visible
- Download link available
- Degrada graceful a link de texto en navegadores sin JS / Reader Mode

title: Review copyright and licensing for sample textos
Add CF Web Analytics to existing pages (index, episodes)
Add Cloudflare Web Analytics for visit metrics
Build textos pipeline (markdown to HTML, index, template)
Ensure reader mode compatibility for episode pages
Build markdown-to-HTML pipeline for episode pages
Implement responsive episode layout (audio, video embeds, images)
Create episode page template with frontmatter support
Reconcile and archive the completed episode OpenSpec change
description: The add-markdown-episodes OpenSpec task list still marks implemented work incomplete and does not trace current repair tickets, making the specification misleading. Reconcile each task with repository reality, link unresolved requirements to Beads issues, validate the change strictly, and archive it only after required repairs are complete.
design: Relevant path: openspec/changes/add-markdown-episodes/. Follow the openspec-archive workflow only after blockers close. Reconcile tasks against commits and Beads IDs, run strict validation, then archive the deployed change.
acceptance_criteria: Every add-markdown-episodes task is accurately marked complete or linked to an open Beads issue; the implemented behavior and delta spec agree; strict OpenSpec validation passes; the change is archived only after all required dependencies close.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Provide production media and distribution destinations
description:
## Decision needed
The site needs owner-supplied production destinations before hidden listening and subscription controls can be restored: episode audio URLs, Spotify show URL, Apple Podcasts URL, YouTube destination, and a decision whether to publish an RSS feed.

## Rationale
These values are external product facts and cannot be inferred safely from the repository. Keeping controls hidden is preferable to shipping placeholders.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Keep placeholder links: rejected because they mislead production users.
2. Permanently remove distribution controls: valid if destinations will not exist, but requires owner confirmation.
3. Restore only confirmed destinations and omit the rest: preferred.

## Human input
Provide authoritative URLs, identify unavailable destinations, and choose either generate RSS or omit RSS.
design: HITL only: record authoritative destinations in the issue response. Do not edit source files until URLs and the RSS decision are supplied. A later implementation issue may restore only destinations confirmed here.
acceptance_criteria: Authoritative production URLs are recorded for each available destination; unavailable destinations are explicitly marked unavailable; the RSS decision is recorded; implementation tickets can consume these values without guessing.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Make episode date formatting independent of build timezone
description:
## Problem
Date-only frontmatter values are parsed as UTC instants and formatted in the process timezone, so local builds west of UTC can display the previous calendar day.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Build or call the formatter with TZ=UTC for 2026-07-21.
2. Repeat with TZ=America/Argentina/Buenos_Aires.
3. Compare the displayed calendar dates.
design: Relevant file: scripts/build-episodes.mjs. Validate a strict YYYY-MM-DD shape and format calendar dates without local-time conversion, either with UTC explicitly or direct calendar components. Add a Node test run under TZ=UTC and TZ=America/Argentina/Buenos_Aires; include it in just check.
acceptance_criteria: The same date-only input renders the same calendar date under UTC and America/Argentina/Buenos_Aires; date validation enforces the supported input shape; tests exercise at least one timezone west of UTC.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Support the Org syntax already used by site content
description: The custom Org converter supports only headings, tables, unordered lists, links, and paragraphs, while tracked content uses emphasis, bold/fixed-width syntax, ordered lists, and table headers. Unsupported syntax currently appears literally or loses structure.
design: Relevant files: scripts/org-to-html.mjs plus paginas/*.org, programa/*.org, and materiales/*.org as fixtures. Implement only syntax present in tracked content: emphasis/bold/fixed-width text, ordered lists, and semantic table headers. Add focused converter tests and include them in just check.
acceptance_criteria: Generated pages correctly render every Org construct currently used in paginas/, programa/, and materiales/; tables expose semantic header cells; parser tests cover escaping and links alongside emphasis and list handling; unsupported syntax is documented or rejected clearly.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Escape generated template values by default
description:
## Problem
Episode and Org template renderers interpolate metadata directly into HTML text, quoted attributes, and URLs. Quotes can corrupt output and untrusted values can inject markup or unsafe links.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Add an episode metadata value containing a quote, ampersand, or angle brackets.
2. Run `just build`.
3. Inspect the generated attribute or text node and observe unescaped interpolation.
design: Relevant files: scripts/build-episodes.mjs, scripts/build-org-pages.mjs, scripts/episode-template.html, scripts/org-template.html. Introduce escaped text/attribute interpolation and explicit raw-body placeholders; validate URL schemes separately. Add table-driven Node tests for quotes, ampersands, angle brackets, javascript: URLs, and valid rendered body HTML. Verification is exposed through just check.
acceptance_criteria: Text and attribute substitutions are context-appropriately escaped by default; already-rendered body HTML uses an explicit raw insertion path; URL-bearing fields accept only intended schemes; tests cover ampersands, quotes, angle brackets, and malicious-looking values without breaking valid Markdown/Org output.
notes: Source: Rule of 5 codebase review, Amp thread https://ampcode.com/threads/T-019f8c1f-dd51-74ff-bae6-24e4b6abc632

title: Refine navigation labels (Contacto → Sugerir un cuento)
description: Rename 'Contacto' in main navigation to 'Sugerir un cuento' to better communicate the community function.
acceptance_criteria:
- Nav item reads 'Sugerir un cuento'
- Page title/heading updated accordingly
- URL can remain /contacto/ or change to /sugerir/
notes: Pendiente: decidir si la URL cambia a /sugerir/ o queda /contacto/

title: Design empty-state fallbacks for search/filter
description: Design and implement clear fallback states when search or filters return no results — with recommendations instead of blank page.
acceptance_criteria:
- Zero-results shows friendly message
- Suggested top episodes displayed
- Clear filter / try-again button
- Works with search.cljs

title: Add episode cover artwork / thumbnails
description: Add episode cover art or thumbnail images to episode cards for visual scannability. Requires sourcing/creating images and updating squint.edn copy-resources.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each episode has a thumbnail image
- Images are optimized (small file size)
- squint.edn includes image extensions in :copy-resources
- Alt text provided for a11y
- Responsive layout

title: Add metadata badges to episode listings
description:
Add visual metadata badges to episode cards: duration, author, genre/format — so users can scan the archive without reading full descriptions.

Metadata source: stored in episode org file frontmatter (author, genre, duration). The org→HTML build pipeline extracts at build time and includes in the search index JSON. Badges are rendered from this index at page load via search.cljs or direct template injection.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each episode shows: duration badge, author badge, genre badge
- Metadata sourced from org file frontmatter (author, genre, duration)
- Schema defined and extracted at build time into search index JSON
- Badges are visually distinct (icons + text)
- Responsive layout
- Works with existing episode templates

title: Add quick-filter chips for archive browsing
description: Add one-tap filter chips (e.g. #Borges, #Cortázar, #Fantástico) to allow rapid filtering of the episode archive without typing.
acceptance_criteria:
- Filter chips visible on /episodios/ page
- Clicking a chip filters episodes client-side
- Multiple chips can be active (AND/OR logic)
- Clear filter option
- Works with existing search infrastructure

title: Improve print stylesheet (@media print hide media, page URL footer)
Add print button and improved print stylesheets
Add EPUB generation for Kindle/e-reader compatibility
Add frontmatter validation in build step
Generate episode index page from markdown sources
Add site-wide search functionality
Implement persistent bottom audio bar
description: Implement a sticky bottom audio player that persists across page navigations so playback isn't interrupted when users browse the site.
acceptance_criteria:
- Audio continues playing during navigation
- Sticky bottom bar with play/pause, progress, duration
- Requires client-side routing or iframe/service worker approach
- Graceful fallback when JS disabled

title: Add seasonal theming (CSS variables + JS date detection)
Add scheduled and cancelled status to episodes
